response: Japan, with a population of 125 million, boasts a robust economy valued at a GDP of $4.2 trillion, reflecting a modest growth rate of 1.1% amid challenges like an unemployment rate of 2.6% and inflation at 3.2%. The country grapples with a high public debt-to-GDP ratio of 263%, yet it maintains strong global trade ties, exporting goods worth $756 billion annually. Additionally, Japan enjoys one of the world's highest life expectancies at 84.6 years, underscoring its advanced healthcare and quality of life.
